title: Copying certs doesn't work if certs are in subdirectories
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

A freshly added feature implemented in #12709 doesn't support keys in subdirectories within `certs.d`.   
See example structure in the [docker documentation](https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/certificates/):  

```
    /etc/docker/certs.d/
    └── my-https.registry.example.com          <-- Hostname without port
       ├── client.cert
       ├── client.key
       └── ca.crt
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
➜  ~ podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
WARN[0025] Unable to read cert file read /Users/user/.config/docker/certs.d/docker.company.com: is a directory
WARN[0025] Unable to read cert file read /Users/user/.config/docker/certs.d/test.docker.company.com: is a directory
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Directories have been ignored.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Directories should be copied.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.0.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body:
Care to open a PR to fix this?
@flouthoc PTAL
I was thinking of doing that, I hope I'll be able to do that this week.
Oh nice finding. It should be a small change. @aklajnert Should i assign this to you ? No pressure you can assign it back to me if you are busy.

@flouthoc - please assign it to me. I should be able to work on it until the end of this week, but I'm a bit rusty with Go so we'll see how it'll turn out.  

If I'll manage to fix it, is there a chance that the fix will make it into `4.0`?
@aklajnert Yes sure afaik this will be in `4.0`. @mheon could tell this better though.
Yes we need this quick (End of month at latest).
Assuming this lands in the next ~3 weeks it should make it into 4.0
